bypasses.reason: Unsets isDefault on all other payment methods for the client when creating a new default. Cross-entity batch operation: markAsDefault/markNotDefault Manifest commands exist but operate on individual records. The updateMany clears multiple siblings in one query — performing N Manifest commands per request would be O(n) roundtrips vs O(1). Pattern: clearOthers → markAsDefault.
bypasses.whyRuntimeNotRequired: Batch updateMany is a cross-entity housekeeping operation with no domain semantics beyond 'ensure at most one default'. The individual record mutations (markAsDefault/markNotDefault) ARE governed; this is a companion bulk-clear.

bypasses.reason: Unsets isDefault on all other payment methods for the client before marking the target as default. Same cross-entity batch pattern as the POST route. ClearOthers → markAsDefault.
bypasses.whyRuntimeNotRequired: Batch updateMany is a cross-entity housekeeping operation with no domain semantics beyond 'ensure at most one default'. The individual record mutations (markAsDefault/markNotDefault) ARE governed.

bypasses.reason: Batch update of costCalculatedAt timestamp on all ingredients for a recipe version after cost recalculation. This is a metadata-tracking field recording when costs were last computed, not a governed domain property. The actual cost mutation (RecipeVersion.updateCosts) IS governed via Manifest runtime. Using N individual RecipeIngredient.update Manifest commands for a batch timestamp stamp would be O(n) roundtrips for a pure bookkeeping operation with no business rules to enforce.
bypasses.whyRuntimeNotRequired: costCalculatedAt is an infrastructure timestamp, not a domain property. No state transitions, business rules, or governance policies apply to it. The only governed mutation in this route is RecipeVersion.updateCosts which already goes through Manifest runtime.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: Filter scoped to tenantId + recipeVersionId + deletedAt: null
